meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve no understanding of APIs (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome no understanding of APIs. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: no understanding of APIs

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Master API Integration
content.seo_description: Overcome the fear of APIs by building real-world projects. A step-by-step guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to level up your dev career.
content.answer_block: Stop treating APIs as black boxes. By building a portfolio project that fetches, transforms, and displays live data, you bridge the gap between theory and production. Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to iterate on endpoints. Mastery comes from shipping, not just reading documentation.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks staring at documentation, feeling like an imposter because the world of REST, JSON, and headers feels like a foreign language. The gap between your local 'Hello World' and a production-ready SaaS feels like a canyon, and that anxiety is the only thing standing between you and your first engineering role.

But here is the secret: every senior dev started exactly where you are. APIs aren't magic; they are just conversations between computers. We are going to dismantle that barrier by building a high-value portfolio project that forces you to interact with live data, turning your confusion into a competitive advantage that recruiters actually care about.

steps.title: Define Your Data Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, define the shape of the data you need. This clarifies your requirements and prevents scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Architect. Analyze a target public API (e.g., CoinGecko or GitHub) and generate a TypeScript interface representing the core data object we need to fetch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a types.ts file with the interface defined by the architect prompt'.

steps.title: Scaffold the API Client
steps.description: Create a clean, modular service layer to handle requests, keeping your UI logic separate from your networking logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust API service module using Axios. Include error handling, request interceptors for headers, and a mock fallback for development environments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the API service using the generated TypeScript interface and add error logging'.

steps.title: Implement Data Transformation
steps.description: API responses are rarely ready for the UI. Map raw data to your application's specific needs to ensure type safety.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a transformation utility function that maps raw API response objects to our clean application domain models.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the service to use the transformer utility before returning data to the components'.

steps.title: Build the Interactive UI
steps.description: Connect your service layer to your frontend components, implementing loading states and empty states.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a React component that fetches data on mount, displays a skeleton loader during requests, and handles API errors gracefully.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the main dashboard component and integrate the API service hook'.

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push your work to a live environment to prove the integration works in the real world.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration file and a brief README explaining the API integration architecture for my portfolio.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize deployment config and generate project documentation'.

pro_tips: Always wrap your API calls in a try-catch block and log the full response headers during development; they often contain rate-limit info.
Use Postman or Hoppscotch to test your API endpoints before writing a single line of code in your app.
Never hardcode API keys. Use environment variables (.env) and add them to your .gitignore immediately.
Implement a 'stale-while-revalidate' strategy to make your app feel lightning-fast even on slow connections.
Treat your API documentation as your source of truth; if the docs aren't clear, don't guess—use console.log(response) to inspect the object.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, add a 'Rate Limit' counter to your UI to show recruiters you understand production constraints.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to stop fearing APIs and start building the software that will define your career. Don't let your code stay on your local machine—get it into the world where it belongs. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
